title: Divide with visuals
description: Practice basic division using various visuals, such as arrays.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $10$ jumping monkeys. We want to divide them equally onto $2$ beds.

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

**How many monkeys will be on each bed?** 

$10\div 2 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] monkeys
hints:
We can put a monkey on each bed until all the monkeys are on a bed:

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

How many monkeys will be on each bed?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\redD5$ monkeys will be on each bed. 

$10 \div 2 = \redD5$

options.alt: One row of 10 monkeys.
One empty box labeled Bed 1. To the right is another empty box labeled Bed 2.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

There are $20$ flowers. We want to divide them equally into $4$ vases.




[[☃ image 2]]

**How many flowers will be in each vase?** 

$20\div 4 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] flowers
hints:
We can put a flower in each vase until all the flowers are in a vase:

[[☃ image 3]]
[[☃ image 2]]



How many flowers will be in each vase?

[[☃ image 1]]



$\maroonD5$ flowers will be in each vase. 

$20 \div 4 = \maroonD5$

options.alt: Two rows of flowers with 10 flowers in each row.
One row of 4 empty rectangles. The rectangles are labeled Vase 1, Vase 2, Vase 3, and Vase 4.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $18$ apples. We want to divide them equally into $3$ baskets.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**How many apples will be in each basket?** 

$18\div 3 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] apples
hints:
We can put an apple in each basket until all the apples are in a basket:



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]


How many apples will be in each basket?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\redD6$ apples will be in each basket. 

$18 \div 3 = \redD6$

options.alt: 18 apples are arranged in 2 rows of 9 apples each.
3 empty groups represented by rectangles are labeled basket 1, basket 2, and basket 3.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $24$ bananas. We want to divide them equally into $3$ baskets.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]


**How many bananas will be in each basket?** 

$24\div 3 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] bananas
hints:
We can put a banana in each basket until all the bananas are in a basket:

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

How many bananas will be in each basket?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE8$ bananas will be in each basket. 

$24 \div 3 = \goldE8$

options.alt: 24 bananas are arranged in 2 rows of 12 bananas each.
3 empty groups represented by rectangles are labeled basket 1, basket 2, and basket 3.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $24$ pieces of cheese. We want to divide them equally into $6$ bags.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**How many pieces of cheese will be in each bag?** 

$24\div 6 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] pieces
hints:
We can put a piece of cheese in each bag until all the pieces are in a bag:

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

How many pieces will be in each bag?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE4$ pieces of cheese will be in each bag. 

$24 \div 6 = \goldE4$

options.alt: 24 cheese triangles are arranged in 2 rows of 12 cheese triangles each.
6 empty groups represented by rectangles are labeled bag 1, bag 2, bag 3, bag 4, bag 5, and bag 6.

assessment_items.question_content:
We can find $15\div3$ by dividing $15$ dots into $3$ equal rows:

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many dots are in each row?**

$15 \div 3 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueE5$ dots in each row:

[[☃ image 1]]
$15 \div 3 = \blueE5$

options.alt: An array of 15 dots arranged in 3 rows of 5 dots each.

[[☃ image 1]]
$48 \div 6 = \blueE8$

options.alt: An array of 48 dots split into 6 rows with the same number of dots in each row.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jim collected $10$ acorns. He wants to divide the acorns into equal groups so that each group has $5$ acorns.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many groups will he make?**

$10 \div 5 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] groups
hints:
Jim can split the acorns up so that there are $5$ acorns in each group:


[[☃ image 1]]

Jim will make $\blueD2$ groups.

$10 \div 5 = \blueD2$

options.alt: A row of 10 acorns.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sue bought $12$ apples. She wants to divide the apples into bags so that each bag has $4$ apples.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many bags will she need?**

$12 \div 4 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] bags
hints:
Sue can split the apples up so that there are $4$ apples in each bag:

[[☃ image 1]]

Sue will need $\blueD3$ bags.

$12 \div 4 = \blueD3$

options.alt: A row of 12 apples.

assessment_items.question_content:
Cookie Monster has $27$ cookies. He wants to divide the cookies into jars so that each jar has $3$ cookies.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many jars will he need?**

$27 \div 3 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] jars
hints:
Cookie Monster can split the cookies up so that there are $3$ cookies in each jar:

[[☃ image 1]]

Cookie Monster will need $\blueD9$ jars.

$27 \div 3 = \blueD9$

options.alt: 27 cookies arranged into 3 equal rows.

assessment_items.question_content:
Spot has $24$ bones in his doghouse. He wants to divide the bones into equal piles so that each pile has $8$ bones.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many piles will he make?**

$24 \div 8 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] piles
hints:
Spot can split the bones up so that there are $8$ bones in each pile:

[[☃ image 1]]

Spot will make $\blueD3$ piles.

$24 \div 8 = \blueD3$

options.alt: 24 bones are arranged in 2 rows of 12 bones each.

assessment_items.question_content:
Farmer Jon has $28$ pigs on his farm. He wants to divide the pigs into equal groups so that each group has $7$ pigs.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many groups will he make?**

$28 \div 7 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] groups
hints:
Farmer Jon can split the pigs up so that there are $7$ pigs in each group:

[[☃ image 1]]

Farmer Jon will make $\blueD4$ groups.

$28 \div 7 = \blueD4$

options.alt: 28 pigs are arranged in 4 rows of 7 pigs each.

assessment_items.question_content:
We can find $30\div5$ by splitting up $30$ dots so that there are $5$ dots in each row:

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many rows are there?**

$30 \div 5 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD6$ equal rows:

[[☃ image 1]]
$30 \div 5 = \blueD6$

options.alt: An array of 30 dots arranged in 6 rows of 5 dots each. The dots in the top row are labeled 1 through 5.
